title: hypericum mutilum var. floribundum
intro: Hypericum mutilum var. floribundum, commonly known as St. John's Wort, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, North America, and Asia. It is a member of the St. John's Wort family and is known for its bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe skin irritations, and support overall health.
